source_pr.title: ddl: a better way to fix data race against #10900
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

a better way to fix data race against #10900

### What is changed and how it works?

use `config.StoreGlobalConfig` instead of hacking golang function.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test
 

Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change

Side effects

 - None

Related changes

 - None

symptoms: ddl: a better way to fix data race against #10900
failure_signature: ddl: a better way to fix data race against #10900
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to a data race around table-lock-related global configuration used by DDL tests. The previous approach swapped global behavior via a shared atomic/global hook, which is racy under concurrent access. This PR stabilizes the tests by using `config.StoreGlobalConfig` with a copied config and restoring it via defer, avoiding racy global mutation patterns.
fix_pattern: Use `config.StoreGlobalConfig` with a local copy + defer restore instead of racy global hooks/atomics when toggling table-lock config in tests.

evidence: evidence: ddl: a better way to fix data race against #10900
